summary:
The most common infection after solid organ transplantations is typically caused by Cytomegalovirus (CMV), which affects various organs including hearts, kidneys, and livers, contributing significantly to both short- and long-term health outcomes. \boxed{\text{Cytomegalovirus}} Agent1 concluded that among all potential causes of infection post-solid organ transplantation, Cytomegalovirus stands out due to its frequent occurrence and wide-ranging impacts on patients' recovery and overall well-being. Thus, it can be summarized succinctly:

The primary infectious risk post-solids organ transplantation involves **cytomegalovirus**, affecting diverse organs with substantial implications for patient prognosis. \boxed{\text{cycomegalovirus}}
